summary: B11-race genuinely pins bert's atomic commitQuota (db.transaction + SELECT COUNT FOR UPDATE) and is a REAL race, not a false-GREEN. M1 (revert to non-atomic count-then-insert) drives finalCount=51 RED, deterministic x3. The 51 (vs serialization's 50) proves two distinct pooled connections = real concurrency. N-way probe: 5 racers @49 -> exactly 50 (FOR UPDATE gate not 2-specific). Boundary @50->50, @48-2x->50. No quota-on-timeout reintroduced (B5-red GREEN). 58/58 GREEN; tree clean.

refers.why: B11-race only tests 2-way concurrency at count=49. zoe's throwaway N-way/boundary probe (5-way @49/@46, @50 single+2x, @48 2x) all PASS on the fix but is not retained. 2-way is sufficient to pin the binding boundary race; optional to fold an N-way variant into the suite for regression durability. Breadth, not correctness.

Atomic mechanism is SELECT COUNT(*) ... FOR UPDATE in a REPEATABLE-READ trx anchored on idx_ai_command_log_user_time (no new migration). InnoDB range/gap-lock correctness on the existing composite index is an arch concern; empirically the N-way probe shows correct serialization.
